home.title: Welcome to Villa Vredestein
home.p1: Built in 1906, Villa Vredestein breathes the charm of a bygone era. High ceilings, original details and a soul you feel the moment you step inside.
home.p2: Yet this is not a house frozen in time. Behind the nostalgic facade lies a fresh spirit. We modernise with respect for the past, so this house not only preserves memories but also makes room for new stories.
home.p3: Original elements are preserved wherever possible, not out of nostalgia, but out of love for craftsmanship. This creates a harmony between old and new.
home.p4: Villa Vredestein carries the past with pride and looks to the future. Discover what makes this unique place so special.
home.imageAlt: Front facade of Villa Vredestein in Driebergen

blogs.title: Villa Vredestein: From Run-Down Student House to Restored Pride
blogs.summary: Built in 1906, used as a student house for years, now a work in progress. The honest story of how Villa Vredestein began a second life in 2020.
content: On Hoofdstraat in Driebergen-Rijsenburg stands a property that still has its best years ahead of it. Villa Vredestein, built in 1906 as a reformed-Christian guesthouse, is currently experiencing a second life. That life began in 2020, in the middle of a global pandemic.

When we bought the property, it was far from the villa it had once been. For years it had served as a student house for seven students from IVA, the private automotive school in Driebergen. The walls had seen it, the floors had felt it, and the character of the building was buried deep under layers of neglect. But those who looked closely saw what lay beneath. We saw potential.

The renovation is on a scale you only understand when you are in the middle of it. From cellar to attic, from the driveway to the large barn planned in the back garden, everything is being tackled, everything is being restored. The single-brick walls, the high ceilings, the original wooden floors: these are details you cannot replicate, you can only save them if you take the time and have the patience.

And that renovation does not always go according to plan. There are disappointments, moments when you open something up and find what you would rather not have found. But there are also the most wonderful surprises: moments when the property gives something back that you did not expect. That interplay is what makes it special. It makes it honest.

Villa Vredestein is not a monument; it is a work in progress. A property with more than a century of history on its shoulders, now being returned step by step to the state it always deserved. From reformed guesthouse to student house to what it is becoming now: a place to be proud of.

blogs.title: The History
blogs.summary: Originally a stately retreat far from the city bustle. Over the years it became a place of connection and growth.
content: In the early twentieth century, Driebergen was a popular destination for wealthy Amsterdammers and Utrechters seeking a country retreat away from the hustle and bustle of the city. Villa Vredestein was built in 1906 as exactly such a retreat, a place of rest, nature and reflection.

Over the years the property has been inhabited by various owners, each contributing their own story to the history of the house. From patricians to artists, from businessmen to academics. Villa Vredestein has welcomed people from all walks of life.

Through the decades the property retained its character as a special place: a point of rest amid the changes happening in the world beyond. The thick walls and surrounding greenery always offered a feeling of shelter and a sense of coming home.

Now, more than a century later, Manon and Maxim are bringing a new chapter to this rich story. They carry on the tradition of hospitality and build on the foundations laid by generations before them. The history of Villa Vredestein is alive and still growing.

blogs.title: The Restoration
blogs.summary: Renovated with careful attention to original details. Authentic elements preserved within a contemporary setting.
content: Renovating a historic property is a delicate balance between preservation and renewal. At Villa Vredestein it was clear from the outset that original details had to take centre stage. Not every trace of time was erased, on the contrary, the patina of the past was deliberately embraced.

The restoration began with a thorough inventory of what could be preserved: the original wooden floors, the ornate ceiling mouldings, the impressive doors with their characteristic ironwork. Everything still in good condition was carefully restored and put back in place.

At the same time the villa was equipped with all the modern comforts that residents and guests expect today. Insulation, modern installations and contemporary kitchens were integrated in a way that does not detract from the historic character of the property.

The result is a house that feels both comfortable and authentic. Visitors immediately sense the charm of the historic property while also experiencing the ease of a modern interior. That balance is at the heart of Villa Vredestein's restoration philosophy.

blogs.title: The Surroundings
blogs.summary: Villa Vredestein is located in the green heart of Driebergen, on the edge of the Utrechtse Heuvelrug. Nature, history and tranquility in one place.
content: Driebergen-Rijsenburg is one of the greenest and most peaceful municipalities in the province of Utrecht. Situated on the southern flank of the Utrechtse Heuvelrug, a ridge formed during the last ice age, the surroundings offer an unrivalled combination of nature, tranquility and culture.

From Villa Vredestein you are within minutes of vast forests and heathlands. The Utrechtse Heuvelrug offers endless possibilities for walkers, cyclists and anyone in need of fresh air and open space. The contrast with the bustle of the Randstad is striking and instantly felt.

The village of Driebergen itself is a charming community with a town centre full of characterful buildings, local shops and cosy restaurants. The historic Hoofdstraat breathes the same atmosphere as Villa Vredestein itself: old but lively, traditional yet open to the new.

Utrecht city is just a short train ride away, as are Amsterdam and other major cities. Villa Vredestein thus offers the best of both worlds: a quiet, green environment to unwind in, with the energy of the city always within easy reach.

blogs.title: Maxim & Manon
blogs.summary: Built with patience, craftsmanship and a generous love of travel. The story of Manon and Maxim.
content: Villa Vredestein is the life's work of Maxim and Manon, built with patience, craftsmanship and a generous love of faraway places.

Their paths crossed at KLM. Maxim flies intercontinental routes; Manon worked on the ground, both feet firmly on the tarmac. That combination of distant horizons and a solid foundation runs through everything they build together.

Every trip Maxim takes brings something new home. Chinese tea blends, architectural inspiration from cities far away, fabric he has specially processed in India. Those international influences are woven by hand into every corner of the house, from the sofa in the living room to the details you only notice on a second look.
While Maxim builds, Manon shapes the story and the experience around it. Together they create a place where familiar faces and passing guests feel welcome from the very first moment.

Villa Vredestein is a work in progress, with a ten-year plan, and that is exactly the point. Each season adds a new layer of refinement. What never changes is the warmth with which guests are welcomed, and the smell of freshly made pancakes or Italian pasta that drifts through the house.
Welcome to Villa Vredestein. A home in the making, with plenty of room for your story.

blogs.title: The Book
blogs.summary: Villa Vredestein in pictures. The history. The renovation and restoration of this remarkable property, documented by Manon Keeman.
content: Villa Vredestein has a story bigger than words alone. That is why I decided to capture it in a book, not just in text, but above all in images. A document of what it was, what it has become, and what that process looked like up close.

The book takes you through the history of the property: from its construction in 1906, the successive residents and the decades that left traces in the walls, floors and details you only notice when you really look closely.

A large part of the book is dedicated to the restoration. Not the polished end result, but the real work: the decisions, the surprises behind walls, the choices between preserving and renewing. Photos, sketches and notes that together show the process as it truly was.
The book is still in the making. Want to stay informed when it is released, or interested in reserving a copy? Get in touch via the contact form. We would love to hear from you!

blogs.title: Carpe Diem Design
blogs.summary: Not just a name. Carpe Diem Design grows from a personal story — and is open to anyone who wants to build.
content: It started with one word from his grandfather. No logo, no brand — just a signature on a birthday card. A way of living that travelled from generation to generation.
When Maxim's father turned out to be dying at 62, Maxim had just landed. Someone pressed a can into his hands. Carpe Diem was written on it. That evening they gave a speech. They raised a toast.
Since then, it has never just been a motto.

Carpe Diem Design is what grows out of that. An idea for a place where people build. Developers who want to make something tangible. Makers who are done with half-finished work. No showroom, no design studio — something far more concrete than that.
What exactly it will become? That is still open. And that is exactly the point.

Maxim built his own café racer from loose parts into a running motorcycle, together with people from across the world. That is how he thinks about this project. Not everything drawn up before it begins — but building together towards something that would otherwise never have existed.
Interested in thinking along or building together? Get in touch.

blogs.title: Visit & Inspiration
blogs.summary: Discover the stories, charm and ambitions of Villa Vredestein. Be inspired by the process of restoration and living.
content: Villa Vredestein is more than an address. It is a living story. Every day new pages are written by the people who live, work and stay there. Through this website and social media we love to share that story with a wider audience.

On Instagram (@villa.vredestein) we document life in and around the villa. From the first morning sun on the facade to spontaneous moments in the garden, we show how special everyday life in this historic property can be. Inspiration for anyone who wants to start their own project one day.

The villa also serves as a source of inspiration for people interested in historic real estate, interior design or sustainable renovation. We regularly receive questions from people working on similar projects and are happy to share our experiences.

Would you like to know more, pay a visit or simply get in touch? Send us a message via the contact form or reach out via WhatsApp. We are always open to a good conversation about Villa Vredestein, about living, about dreams, and how to make them reality.

footer.tagline: A historic villa from 1906 in the heart of Driebergen-Rijsenburg.
